title: [Bug]: podman failing to build image using ubi9/go-toolset, but docker build succeeds
body:
### Issue Description

I have a simple Go project with a Dockerfile, which can be found here: https://github.com/devfile-samples/devfile-sample-go-basic

The [Dockerfile](https://github.com/devfile-samples/devfile-sample-go-basic/blob/main/docker/Dockerfile) uses registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/go-toolset:latest as a base image. 

When I try to build the project with podman on macOS, I get the following error:
```
go build command-line-arguments: copying /tmp/go-build3766194548/b001/exe/a.out: open main: permission denied
Error: building at STEP "RUN go build -o main ./main.go": while running runtime: exit status 1
```

If I do the same build with Docker, no such error occurs. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install latest podman
2. `git clone https://github.com/devfile-samples/devfile-sample-go-basic`
3. `podman build -f docker/Docker .`

### Describe the results you received

go build command-line-arguments: copying /tmp/go-build1509128078/b001/exe/a.out: open main: permission denied
Error: building at STEP "RUN go build -o main ./main.go": while running runtime: exit status 1


### Describe the results you expected

The podman build succeeds

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

comments.body: I'm not entirely confident this is a Podman issue, but an avenue for opening bugs against UBI wasn't entirely clear to me, so I figured I'd start here to rule out Podman as the problem at the very least
Can you try to disable selinux temporarily (`podman machine ssh sudo setenforce 0`) and try again? That should tell us if it is selinux problem or not.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing because I got no further feedback so I assume it is working now.
